body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager` **Service directory:** `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/` **Triggered by:** commit a40c877d8decd79accea92d741637f7ec88211dd (PR #58123) by @m-nash ## What Changed PR #58123 added a new `ArmClientHostExtensions` class with four public extension methods that register `ArmClient` as a singleton in an `IHostApplicationBuilder`'s service collection using the Azure client factory pattern: - `AddArmClient(host, sectionName)` — registers a singleton `ArmClient` from a configuration section - `AddArmClient(host, sectionName, configureSettings)` — same, with a settings post-processing callback - `AddKeyedArmClient(host, key, sectionName)` — registers a keyed singleton `ArmClient` - `AddKeyedArmClient(host, key, sectionName, configureSettings)` — same, with a settings post-processing callback These are marked `[Experimental("SCME0002")]` and live in `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/src/Extensions/ArmClientHostExtensions.cs`. ## Gaps Found - **README.md has no dependency-injection section.** The README covers manual `new ArmClient(…)` construction and manual authentication but has no guidance for `IHostApplicationBuilder` / .NET Generic Host DI registration. The new extension methods are not referenced anywhere in the README. - **CHANGELOG.md `1.15.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` Features Added section is empty.** The `AddArmClient` / `AddKeyedArmClient` additions were not logged in the upcoming release notes. - **No sample code or test snippet.** There is no `tests/Samples/` directory under `Azure.ResourceManager` and no snippet showing how to call `AddArmClient` in a `Program.cs` / host startup context.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File 1 — `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/CHANGELOG.md`** (edit) Under the `## 1.15.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` heading, replace the empty `### Features Added` section: ```markdown ### Features Added - Added `AddArmClient` and `AddKeyedArmClient` extension methods on `IHostApplicationBuilder` to register `ArmClient` as a singleton in the .NET Generic Host service collection. Configuration is read from the specified `IConfiguration` section name using `ArmClientSettings`. These methods are experimental (diagnostic ID `SCME0002`). ``` **File 2 — `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/README.md`** (edit) Add a new **"Use with dependency injection"** subsection inside the existing `## Getting started` section, after the `### Authenticate the Client` subsection. Content: ```markdown ### Use with dependency injection If your application uses the .NET Generic Host (ASP.NET Core, Worker Service, etc.), you can register `ArmClient` with the DI container using the `AddArmClient` extension on `IHostApplicationBuilder`. This API is currently experimental (diagnostic ID `SCME0002`). ```C# Snippet:ResourceManager_AddArmClient ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Write the sample code in a **new** test file: **File:** `sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/tests/Samples/Sample_HostedService.cs` ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.Tests.Samples { public class Sample_HostedService { [Test] [Experimental("SCME0002")] public void AddArmClientToHost() { #region Snippet:ResourceManager_AddArmClient // Build an IHostApplicationBuilder (e.g. WebApplication.CreateBuilder in ASP.NET Core) IHostApplicationBuilder builder = Host.CreateApplicationBuilder(); // Register ArmClient using the "ArmClient" section of appsettings.json. // The section should contain at minimum a "TenantId" and a "SubscriptionId". builder.AddArmClient("ArmClient"); // Services that depend on ArmClient can now receive it via constructor injection. IHost host = builder.Build(); #endregion Assert.IsNotNull(host); } } } ``` - **Snippet name:** `ResourceManager_AddArmClient` - **README location:** the new `### Use with dependency injection` subsection added in Step 1 ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all six required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). Only the `Getting started` section needs the new DI subsection. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 resourcemanager` — injects the `ResourceManager_AddArmClient` snippet from the test file into the README placeholder 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 resourcemanager` — not strictly needed here (no public API surface changed by docs), but run to confirm API listings are clean 5. `dotnet format sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [ResourceManager] Docs: Document AddArmClient IHostApplicationBuilder extensions
